rows:
(i) Although the use is a non-residential land use, the
building must be of residential appearance, in keeping
with the surrounding environment, and not detract from
the amenity of adjoining properties.
(ii) The building and any additions must:
• be consistent in style with any existing development
on site; and/or

(i) All car parking is to be provided on-site; verge
parking is not permitted.
(ii) Car parks should, where practicable, be
located at the rear of the building and the
location clearly sign-posted. Car parking in
front of the consulting rooms should be
minimised in order to maintain a residential
streetscape.

(i) Car parks shall be designed in accordance with
Australian Standards AS 2890.1 and/or AS
2890.2 as amended from time to time.
(ii) Tandem car parking will be considered for
employee parking only and must be clearly
designated as such.

(i) Vehicles are required to enter and exit the site
in forward gear.
(ii) A maximum of two 3 metre wide crossovers or
one 6 metre wide crossover is permitted.

(i) A footpath must be provided from the carpark
and the street to the building entrance.

(i) A minimum of 8% of the area of a lot shall be
landscaped.
(ii) The landscaped area shall include a minimum strip of
1.5 metres wide adjacent to all street boundaries.

(i) Any landscaped area shall have a minimum width of 1.0
metre and distributed in areas of not less than 4.0
square metres.

(i) Shade trees shall be provided and maintained in
uncovered car parks at the rate of one tree for every
four car parking bays.

(i) The verge areas of all consulting rooms are required to
be suitably landscaped, reticulated and maintained to
discourage patrons from parking on the verge. The
verge is not permitted to be paved or sealed as this
would encourage its use for parking.
